   154	## Phase 8 — Cross-process reference
   155	
   156	Extracts the dispatch loop into separate processes over a wire protocol. First point at which a third-party component in any language could participate.
   157	
   158	**Units:**
   159	
   160	- **8a — complete.** [`spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md`](../spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md) pins the HTTP binding for chapters 4, 5, 6 §3.4, and 8 §§1.1–2.1. The `eden-wire` package ships a FastAPI `make_app(store)` server plus an httpx-backed `StoreClient` that satisfies the same `Store` Protocol. `Store.integrate_trial` is now same-value idempotent so HTTP retries are safe; the `Integrator` distinguishes different-SHA divergence (`AtomicityViolation`) from compensable synchronous rejections; `StoreClient` resolves transport-indeterminate failures via read-back. Long-poll and non-blocking polling both bind the §2.1 `subscribe` operation. Errors round-trip as RFC 7807 problem+json with a closed `eden://error/<name>` vocabulary (chapter §7).
   161	- **8b — complete.** Five reference services under [`reference/services/`](../reference/services/): `task-store-server` (uvicorn-hosted `Store`), `orchestrator` (finalize + dispatch + integrate loop driven by a `StoreClient`), and standalone worker hosts `planner`, `implementer`, `evaluator`. Shared scaffolding lives in `reference/services/_common/` (JSON-line logger, SIGTERM stop flag, readiness probe, common argparse, scripted role profiles, `seed_bare_repo`). `eden-dispatch` exposes `run_orchestrator_iteration` as its orchestrator-side entry point so the standalone orchestrator drives only that half; the in-process `run_experiment` driver was kept through 8b and removed in 8c. `eden-wire.make_app(store, *, shared_token=…)` and `eden-wire.StoreClient(…, token=…)` add reference-only bearer-token auth; misauth surfaces as `eden://reference-error/unauthorized` (HTTP 401), kept out of the normative `eden://error/…` vocabulary by design. The new informative §12 in [`spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md`](../spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md) documents the scheme. Implementer host writes real git commits (single-parent or merge, honoring `proposal.parent_commits`) on `work/*` branches in the bare repo the orchestrator integrates from. A `pytest.mark.e2e` test forks all five processes on an ephemeral port and drives a 3-trial experiment to quiescence over real HTTP + SQLite + git in ~3 seconds.
   162	- **8c — complete.** In-process dispatch path removed: `eden-dispatch.run_experiment` and the Phase 5 / 7b in-process end-to-end tests in `reference/packages/eden-dispatch/tests/test_end_to_end.py` are deleted; `eden-dispatch` keeps `run_orchestrator_iteration` plus the scripted workers as its public surface. The lifecycle-reconstruction invariant from the deleted file moved into `reference/services/orchestrator/tests/test_e2e.py` as a `_reconstruct_lifecycle` fold over the post-teardown `SqliteStore.read_range()`. The malformed-success → `validation_error` routing case (`test_store_hardening`) was rewritten to drive `run_orchestrator_iteration` directly through the legitimate `Store` API. The real-subprocess `pytest.mark.e2e` test from 8b is now the only end-to-end coverage path.

   177

Session cookie is `itsdangerous`-signed with HttpOnly + SameSite=Lax + Path=/, plus per-session CSRF token validated on every mutating route.

Cross-cutting prerequisite: `eden_dispatch.sweep_expired_claims` runs once per orchestrator iteration so abandoned UI claims are recovered automatically.

Orphaned `starting` trials auto-recover through the chunk-1 expired-claim sweeper's `_base.reclaim` composite-commit.
   181	- **9d — complete.** Evaluator module shipped, mounted unconditionally (no CLI flag): claim with TTL (the `trial_id` is read from `task.payload.trial_id` and stashed in an in-process `_CLAIMS` dict, never round-tripping through the request surface), draft form rendering trial-under-evaluation context (trial_id / branch / commit_sha / parent_commits, plus the implementer-set `trial.description` and `trial.artifacts_uri` per §3.2 step 3, with the chunk-9c scheme allowlist + the same ≤ 1 MiB confinement applied to `trial.artifacts_uri`) + proposal context + one input per metric in `experiment_config.metrics_schema` typed by `MetricType`. Submission validates (status ∈ {success, error, eval_error}; per-metric type rules including the wire-legal integer form `1.0` per `02-data-model.md` §1.3 and rejection of non-finite reals; `status=success` requires ≥ 1 metric value), then runs `store.submit` with retry-before-orphan + read-back where `WrongToken` / `ConflictingResubmission` short-circuit, `InvalidPrecondition` re-renders the form (fixable), and **`IllegalTransition` falls through to read-back** so a "we won, response lost, orchestrator already terminalized" sequence correctly classifies as success rather than orphan. The chunk-9c trust-boundary helper was generalized to `_read_inline_artifact(uri, artifacts_dir)`; `read_proposal_rationale` is a thin wrapper and a new `read_trial_artifact` covers the trial-side surface.
   182	- **9e — complete.** Admin / observability surface shipped under `/admin/*`: read-only task / trial / event tables (filterable, capped, with claim-age + claim-expired badges and an orphaned-starting-trial badge), a per-task detail page exposing operator `reclaim` via `Store.reclaim(task_id, "operator")` per [`spec/v0/04-task-protocol.md`](../spec/v0/04-task-protocol.md) §5.1 (separate "reclaim" / "force-reclaim (replays work)" UI variants for `claimed` vs `submitted`; closed allowlist of banner outcomes), a per-trial detail page whose related-events filter unions `event.data.trial_id == trial_id` with task-id matches for the implement task that produced this trial and any evaluate task that references it, ordered by replay-index position (the `event_id` factory is pluggable and not a reliable ordering contract), and a `work/*` ref GC page (when `--repo-path` is set) that classifies refs by exact `trial.branch` equality — not by parsing the ref name — and offers CAS-guarded `repo.delete_ref(ref_name, expected_old_sha=…)` deletion only for terminal-and-handled trials whose `commit_sha` matches the live ref SHA, plus orphan-ref deletion when no trial owns the branch. Auth-first POST discipline matches the existing modules (`get_session` runs before `csrf_ok`, so unauth → 303 `/signin`, missing-CSRF → 403). `make_app` adds an `@app.exception_handler(eden_storage.errors.NotFound)` so raised storage/wire `NotFound` renders the 404 error page (the prior handler only caught HTTP 404 *responses*).

   192	## Phase 10 — Reference Compose stack
   193	
   194	Everything runs locally via `docker compose up`. Equivalent to the old microservices plan's Milestone 1, now explicitly "one valid deployment topology."
   195	
   196	**Units:**
   197	
   198	- **10a — complete.** [`reference/compose/`](../reference/compose/) ships a Compose stack standing up Postgres (`postgres:16.6-alpine`, reserved for `PostgresStore` consumption in 10b — *not* used by Gitea), Gitea (`gitea/gitea:1.22.6-rootless`, headless), and a one-shot `blob-init` (`busybox:1.36.1`) whose mount triggers creation of the `eden-blob-data` named volume (a top-level `volumes:` declaration alone does *not* create the volume — verified empirically). Long-running services declare `depends_on: { blob-init: service_completed_successfully }` so `compose up --wait` interprets the one-shot's exit-0 as success. Required secrets use `${VAR:?msg}` so a missing `.env` fails loudly; defaults dodge the well-known host-side ports (5433/3001/2222). A new `compose-smoke` CI job runs [`reference/compose/healthcheck/smoke.sh`](../reference/compose/healthcheck/smoke.sh) which asserts named-volume existence, blob-init exit code via `docker inspect`, Postgres connectivity, and Gitea's `/api/v1/version` response. 10a's blob-volume contract is "volume exists" only; ownership/permissions are 10d's responsibility.
   199	- **10b + 10c — complete.** Six EDEN reference services (`task-store-server`, `orchestrator`, `planner-host`, `implementer-host`, `evaluator-host`, `web-ui`) plus a setup-time `eden-repo-init` profile-`setup` service are wired into [`reference/compose/compose.yaml`](../reference/compose/compose.yaml), backed by a single shared `eden-reference:dev` image (multi-stage `uv sync --frozen --no-dev --all-packages`). New `eden-storage.PostgresStore` consumes the 10a-deferred Postgres backend (psycopg v3, SERIALIZABLE per-op) and is conformance-tested across the same Protocol scenarios as `InMemoryStore`/`SqliteStore` via a new `python-test-postgres` CI job. New [`reference/scripts/setup-experiment/setup-experiment.sh`](../reference/scripts/setup-experiment/setup-experiment.sh) bootstraps a runnable stack from an experiment-config YAML in one shot: generates/preserves secrets, builds the shared image, runs `eden-repo-init` synchronously to seed the bare-repo volume, captures the seed SHA, and writes a complete `.env`. Operator workflow: `setup-experiment.sh <config> --experiment-id <id> && docker compose --env-file .env up -d --wait`. `task-store-server` gains a URL-based `--store-url` flag (`:memory:` / `sqlite:///<path>` / `postgresql://…` / bare-path-for-compat); the web-ui gains an unauthenticated `/healthz` endpoint for compose healthchecks. **Roadmap deltas committed with this chunk:** (1) one shared image, not per-service-image, because the workspace shares most deps and N images is a Phase-13/Helm concern; (2) experiment-specific image deferred to 10d (its only consumer is the LLM implementer's sandbox); (3) control-plane registration deferred to Phase 12 (no control plane yet); (4) workers integrate with Gitea as their actual git remote deferred to a follow-up sub-chunk after 10d (workers currently use a Compose-shared bare-repo volume; HTTPS-against-Gitea is its own refactor). The extended `compose-smoke` CI job runs setup-experiment, asserts the seeded ref before `compose up`, brings up the full stack with `--wait`, waits up to 180s for the orchestrator to exit 0 on quiescence, and verifies the final state on the wire endpoint: ≥3 `trial.integrated` events, ≥9 `task.completed` events (3 plan + 3 implement + 3 evaluate), and ≥3 `task.completed` events whose `task_id` begins with `plan-` (each seeded plan task reached a terminal state). Branch protection is **not** updated to require `python-test-postgres` in this chunk — that lands in a follow-up after the job has a few clean runs.
   200	- **10d — complete (subprocess pass).** Each worker host gains `--mode subprocess` that invokes a user-supplied command (`plan_command` / `implement_command` / `evaluate_command`) read from the experiment-config YAML. The planner subprocess is **long-running** with a JSON-line stdin/stdout protocol (so user code can hold accumulating LLM context across plan tasks); the implementer + evaluator subprocesses are **per-task short-lived** with cwd = a per-task git worktree under `<worktrees-dir>/<container_hostname>/<task_id>/`. The implementer flow honors [`spec/v0/03-roles.md`](../spec/v0/03-roles.md) §3.2 step 1 — `Store.create_trial(status="starting")` runs **before** any repository write. Cross-host worktree races are eliminated by construction: each host has a private subdir, startup-time cleanup uses path-scoped `git worktree remove --force` (not the repo-global `git worktree prune`). Worker-side failure context (timeout, malformed outcome JSON) goes to the host's structured logger only; the chapter-3 submission shapes carry no free-form `description`. Documented as a non-normative reference binding at [`spec/v0/reference-bindings/worker-host-subprocess.md`](../spec/v0/reference-bindings/worker-host-subprocess.md). New fixture scripts (`tests/fixtures/experiment/{plan,implement,eval}.py`) exercise the protocol deterministically; the new `compose-smoke-subprocess` CI job runs end-to-end alongside the existing `compose-smoke`. Compose plumbing is a non-invasive overlay [`reference/compose/compose.subprocess.yaml`](../reference/compose/compose.subprocess.yaml). **Roadmap delta:** container isolation of the subprocess (docker-in-docker, experiment-specific image build, auth-secret mounting) is **deferred to a 10d follow-up sub-chunk** — the subprocess.Popen call is the wrappable layer. Branch protection is not updated to require `compose-smoke-subprocess` in this chunk.
   201	- **10e** — End-to-end Compose integration test exercising the full loop including Web UI, admin-reclaim, and termination.
   202	
   203	**Chunks:** 10a one; 10b + 10c one chunk (dockerize + setup script co-evolve — each image's env vars and entrypoints feed the script); 10d one; 10e one.
   204	
   205	**Non-goals:** k8s (Phase 13); S3 blob backend (Phase 13); Gitea auth (Phase 13).

1	# eden-planner-host
     2	
     3	Reference planner worker: polls a task-store-server for pending `plan` tasks, claims each in turn, drafts proposals using the canonical Phase 8b scripted profile, and submits.
     4	
     5	Proposals carry `parent_commits=(--base-commit-sha,)` so the Integrator's reachability check has a real parent to verify against.
     6	
     7	## Run
     8	
     9	```bash
    10	python -m eden_planner_host \
    11	  --task-store-url http://127.0.0.1:8080 \
    12	  --experiment-id exp-1 \
    13	  --worker-id planner-1 \
    14	  --base-commit-sha <40-hex-sha>
    15	```
    16	
    17	Runs until SIGTERM.
    18	
    19	## Subprocess mode
    20	
    21	Pass `--mode subprocess` plus `--experiment-config <path>`,
    22	`--experiment-dir <path>`, and `--artifacts-dir <path>` to invoke a
    23	user-supplied long-running planner command instead of the scripted
    24	profile. The command string is read from the experiment-config
    25	YAML's `plan_command` key. The subprocess exchanges JSON-line
    26	messages with the host per the
    27	[reference binding](../../../spec/v0/reference-bindings/worker-host-subprocess.md):
    28	
    29	1. Subprocess emits `{"event": "ready"}` once on startup.
    30	2. For each plan task, host writes a `{"event": "plan", ...}` line
    31	   on stdin; subprocess emits any number of `proposal` lines and
    32	   then `{"event": "plan-done", "task_id": …}` (or `plan-error`).
    33	3. cwd = the experiment directory; env carries
    34	   `EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR`.
    35	
    36	Use `--plan-env-file path/to/env` to inject `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` and
    37	similar.

1	# eden-implementer-host
     2	
     3	Reference implementer worker: claims pending `implement` tasks, writes a real commit to the shared bare repo using `GitRepo`, and submits the new SHA. Honors the full `proposal.parent_commits` list — single-parent proposals yield single-parent commits, merge proposals yield merge commits.
     4	
     5	## Run
     6	
     7	```bash
     8	python -m eden_implementer_host \
     9	  --task-store-url http://127.0.0.1:8080 \
    10	  --experiment-id exp-1 \
    11	  --worker-id implementer-1 \
    12	  --repo-path /tmp/eden-bare-repo
    13	```
    14	
    15	Runs until SIGTERM.
    16	
    17	## Subprocess mode
    18	
    19	Pass `--mode subprocess` plus `--experiment-config <path>`,
    20	`--experiment-dir <path>`, and (optionally) `--worktrees-dir
    21	<path>` to invoke a user-supplied per-task implement command
    22	instead of the scripted profile. The command string is read from
    23	the experiment-config YAML's `implement_command` key.
    24	
    25	For each implement task the host:
    26	
    27	1. Generates `trial_id` and persists `Trial(status="starting")`
    28	   before any repo write.
    29	2. Creates a per-task git worktree at `parent_commits[0]`.
    30	3. Writes `<wt>/.eden/task.json`, runs the command with cwd=wt
    31	   and env carrying `EDEN_TASK_JSON` / `EDEN_OUTPUT` /
    32	   `EDEN_WORKTREE` / `EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR`.
    33	4. Reads `<wt>/.eden/outcome.json` (`status` + `commit_sha`),
    34	   validates §3.3 reachability, creates `refs/heads/work/<…>`,
    35	   and submits.
    36	
    37	Worktrees live under `<worktrees-dir>/<container_hostname>/<task_id>/`
    38	so cross-host sweeps cannot collide; see the
    39	[reference binding](../../../spec/v0/reference-bindings/worker-host-subprocess.md).

1	# AGENTS.md
     2	
     3	This file provides guidance to AI agents working with this repository.
     4	
     5	## What this repo is
     6	
     7	EDEN is a **protocol** for directed-code-evolution orchestration, not a single product. The repo contains three distinct kinds of artifact, each with different change discipline:
     8	
     9	| Layer | Path | Authoritative? | Change discipline |

    19	**Phase 10 chunk 10d complete.** Each of the planner / implementer / evaluator hosts gains a new `--mode subprocess` that invokes a user-supplied command from the experiment-config YAML (`plan_command` / `implement_command` / `evaluate_command`) instead of the deterministic Phase-8b scripted profile. The subprocess invocation is via `shell=True` so user expressions like `python3 ${EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR}/plan.py` expand correctly; the host supplies `EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR`, `EDEN_TASK_JSON`, `EDEN_OUTPUT`, and (for implement/eval) `EDEN_WORKTREE`. Planner subprocess is **long-running** with a JSON-line `ready` / `plan` / `proposal` / `plan-done` protocol over stdin/stdout (so user code can hold accumulating LLM context across plan tasks); implementer + evaluator subprocesses are **per-task short-lived** with cwd set to a per-task git worktree, and read/write outcome JSON via the `EDEN_OUTPUT` path. The implementer flow honors [`spec/v0/03-roles.md`](spec/v0/03-roles.md) §3.2 step 1 — `Store.create_trial(status="starting")` runs **before** any repository write, mirroring the chunk-9c web-ui implementer ordering. Worktrees are host-private under `<worktrees-dir>/<container_hostname>/<task_id>/`; startup-time cleanup uses path-scoped `git worktree remove --force` (never repo-global `git worktree prune`), so cross-host races don't exist by construction. The chapter-3 submission shapes carry no free-form `description` field; worker-side failure context (subprocess timeout, malformed outcome JSON, etc.) goes to the host log only. The protocol is documented as a non-normative **reference binding** at [`spec/v0/reference-bindings/worker-host-subprocess.md`](spec/v0/reference-bindings/worker-host-subprocess.md) — a conforming alternative host can either match the shape (drop-in compatibility with the fixture experiment) or pick a different mechanism. Three new fixture scripts ([`tests/fixtures/experiment/plan.py`](tests/fixtures/experiment/plan.py), [`implement.py`](tests/fixtures/experiment/implement.py), [`eval.py`](tests/fixtures/experiment/eval.py)) exercise the protocol deterministically without LLM secrets so the new `compose-smoke-subprocess` CI job can run end-to-end alongside the original `compose-smoke`. The fixture's `*_command` config keys gain `${EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR}/...` prefixes (single source of truth: the experiment YAML, not a CLI flag). Compose plumbing is a non-invasive overlay [`reference/compose/compose.subprocess.yaml`](reference/compose/compose.subprocess.yaml) layered via `compose -f compose.yaml -f compose.subprocess.yaml`; setup-experiment grows `--experiment-dir` (defaults to the directory containing the config's `.eden`) and writes `EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR_HOST` into `.env` for the bind-mount. Container isolation of the user's command (docker-in-docker, experiment-specific image build, auth-secret mounting) is **deferred to a 10d follow-up sub-chunk**; the subprocess.Popen call is the wrappable layer, and the protocol stays unchanged when wrapped. Branch protection is not updated to require `compose-smoke-subprocess` in this chunk (same posture 10a / 10b+10c took for newly-added jobs).

    21	**Phase 10 chunks 10b + 10c complete.** Six EDEN reference services are now dockerized and wired into the Compose stack at [`reference/compose/`](reference/compose/), and a new [`reference/scripts/setup-experiment/setup-experiment.sh`](reference/scripts/setup-experiment/setup-experiment.sh) bootstraps a runnable stack from an experiment-config YAML in one shot. After `bash setup-experiment.sh <config.yaml> --experiment-id <id> && docker compose --env-file .env up -d --wait`, the fixture experiment runs to completion in Compose: orchestrator dispatches three plan tasks → planner-host claims/submits → orchestrator integrates → 3 trials succeed and integrate, with the orchestrator exiting 0 on quiescence (`restart: "on-failure"` so exit-0 sticks; tuned to `--poll-interval 1.0 --max-quiescent-iterations 30` for Compose worker-startup latency). All seven services (six EDEN services + setup-time `eden-repo-init`) share **one** `eden-reference:dev` image (multi-stage build with `uv sync --frozen --no-dev --all-packages` — the `--all-packages` flag is load-bearing because the workspace root pins `tool.uv.package = false` and lists the `eden-*` workspace members only in the `dev` group, which `--no-dev` would otherwise skip). The roadmap delta documenting the per-service-image deviation, the deferral of the LLM-implementer experiment-specific image to 10d, the deferral of control-plane registration to Phase 12, and the deferral of Gitea-as-the-workers'-actual-git-remote to a follow-up sub-chunk after 10d is recorded in the plan and reflected in the chunk's roadmap update. New `eden-storage.PostgresStore` ([`reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/postgres.py`](reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/postgres.py)) is the third backend conforming to the same `Store` Protocol as `InMemoryStore`/`SqliteStore`; backed by `psycopg[binary]>=3.2,<4` with `autocommit=True` + explicit per-op `BEGIN ISOLATION LEVEL SERIALIZABLE READ WRITE` (psycopg's auto-begin would error `ActiveSqlTransaction` on `BEGIN ISOLATION LEVEL`). Schema is byte-for-byte parallel to SQLite (`data text NOT NULL`, not `jsonb` — keeps the read path identical and defers indexing concerns). The new `python-test-postgres` CI job spins up `postgres:16.6-alpine` as a service container and runs the parametrized backend tests against it; the existing `python-test` job continues to run with `EDEN_TEST_POSTGRES_DSN` unset (so the postgres rows skip), keeping it fast. `task-store-server` gains a URL-based `--store-url` flag (`:memory:` / `sqlite:///<path>` / `postgresql://…` / bare-path-for-compat); the deprecated `--db-path` alias survives for one phase. Bare-repo bootstrap is owned **synchronously** by setup-experiment (one canonical command shape: `docker compose build eden-repo-init && docker compose run --rm --no-deps eden-repo-init` — `--no-deps` keeps future dependency edits from accidentally starting Postgres/Gitea just to seed). The seed SHA is captured from stdout and replaced into a placeholder line in `.env` (zero-padded sentinel) — *replaced*, not appended, so `grep -E '^EDEN_BASE_COMMIT_SHA='` returns one definition. The `eden-repo-init` service has `profiles: ["setup"]` so it's invisible to plain `compose up`. Volume permissions are pre-created in the Dockerfile via `RUN mkdir -p /var/lib/eden/{repo,artifacts} && chown -R eden:eden /var/lib/eden` — without this the named volume inherits root ownership from the volume driver and the eden user can't write. Healthchecks for `task-store-server` and `web-ui` use compose-load-time `${VAR}` interpolation (single `$`), not container-shell `$${VAR}`, because the env-file vars aren't propagated into the container's shell environment unless declared under `environment:`; baking the values into the literal command at compose-load is simpler. The web-ui mount is **read-write**, not read-only, because passing `--repo-path` activates the entire implementer module (chunk 9c) which writes refs on submit — read-only would 500. The redundancy with `implementer-host` is intentional (operators pick which they prefer; both race for claim atomicity via `Store.claim` chapter 04 §3). New `web-ui /healthz` endpoint is unauthenticated by design (compose healthchecks must work before any user signs in; reveals only "process is up"). Extended [`reference/compose/healthcheck/smoke.sh`](reference/compose/healthcheck/smoke.sh) (the `compose-smoke` CI job's invariant) now runs setup-experiment, asserts the seeded ref *before* `compose up` via a one-shot probe that reuses the already-built `eden-reference:dev` image (avoiding a floating `latest` tag), waits up to 180s for the orchestrator to exit 0 on quiescence, and verifies the final task-store state on the wire endpoint: ≥3 `trial.integrated` events (full pipeline), ≥9 `task.completed` events (3 plan + 3 implement + 3 evaluate), and ≥3 `task.completed` events whose `task_id` starts with `plan-` (each seeded plan task reached a terminal state). Branch protection is **not** updated to require `python-test-postgres` in this chunk — that lands in a follow-up after the job has a few clean runs (same posture 10a took for `compose-smoke`).

A new informative §12 in chapter 07 documents the scheme. The implementer host writes real git commits (single-parent or merge, honoring `proposal.parent_commits`) on `work/*` branches in the bare repo the orchestrator integrates from. A `pytest.mark.e2e` test in [`reference/services/orchestrator/tests/test_e2e.py`](reference/services/orchestrator/tests/test_e2e.py) forks all five processes on an ephemeral port, drives a 3-trial experiment to quiescence over real HTTP + real SQLite + real git, and tears down with SIGTERM → SIGKILL fallback.

    35	**Phase 8a complete.** `eden-wire` ships an HTTP binding for the chapter 4 / 5 / 6 §3.4 / 8 §§1.1–2.1 operations specified in the new [`spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md`](spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md) chapter. The package exposes a FastAPI ``make_app(store)`` that routes every wire endpoint to a ``Store`` instance, and a ``StoreClient`` that satisfies the same ``Store`` Protocol against the HTTP surface — so existing callers (dispatch driver, integrator) work across the process boundary unchanged. Errors round-trip as RFC 7807 problem+json with a closed vocabulary of ``eden://error/<name>`` types (§7). ``Store.integrate_trial`` is now same-value idempotent (§5 of the chapter); the ``Integrator`` distinguishes different-SHA divergence (``AtomicityViolation``, no ref compensation) from other synchronous rejections (normal §3.4 compensating-delete flow). ``StoreClient.integrate_trial`` reconciles transport-indeterminate failures via read-back: the three outcomes are confirmed success, confirmed divergence (``InvalidPrecondition``), or ``IndeterminateIntegration`` when the server's outcome cannot be determined. Polling + long-poll-style subscribe are both bound (§6). Worker-process extraction, SSE/WebSocket push, and cut-over of in-process paths remain Phase 8b / 8c.

    37	**Phase 6 complete.** The `eden-storage` package ships the `Store` structural interface for the task store, event log, and proposal/trial persistence sides of chapter 8 — collapsed into a single Protocol per §7 implementation latitude. Two conforming backends satisfy it: `InMemoryStore` (fast, non-durable; moved from `eden-dispatch`) and `SqliteStore` (durable across restarts via a WAL-mode SQLite database with `synchronous=FULL`, matching §3.1's crash-survival requirement). The Protocol covers the spec-literal `create_task` / `replay` / `read_range` operations alongside the typed convenience helpers. Both backends share the transition logic in `_base.py`, and the same conformance scenarios are parametrized across both — drift from the Protocol surfaces in tests, not in production. Restart-safety tests close and reopen a SQLite store mid-experiment to confirm state, event log, and claim tokens survive, and a monkey-patched `_apply_commit` failure verifies rollback. The artifact store (§5), `subscribe` streaming (§2.1), and Postgres remain non-goals for Phase 6; they land in Phase 10 / Phase 8 / later respectively.

    39	**Phase 7b complete.** `eden-git` now also ships the `Integrator` that composes `GitRepo` with a `Store` to promote `success` trials per chapter 6. Given a trial with a recorded `commit_sha`, `Integrator.integrate` builds the §3.2 single-commit squash (worker-tip tree plus the eval manifest at `.eden/trials/<trial_id>/eval.json`), writes the `refs/heads/trial/<id>-<slug>` ref via zero-oid CAS, and routes the store's atomic `integrate_trial` write for `trial_commit_sha` and the `trial.integrated` event. On store failure the ref is compensatingly deleted per §3.4, matching the post-promotion reading recorded in [`spec/v0/design-notes/integrator-atomicity.md`](spec/v0/design-notes/integrator-atomicity.md). Re-invocation on an already-promoted trial is a verified no-op (§5.3): ref SHA, squash tree shape, and manifest bytes are re-derived and compared. §2 preconditions (`status == success`, `commit_sha` reachable from `branch` tip), §1.4 reachability, and §2 metrics validity are all enforced up front; the new public `Store.validate_metrics` closes the §2 MUST-NOT-promote clause even if upstream orchestrator validation were bypassed. The spec itself was tightened at §3.4 to make the post-promotion reading explicit. The dispatch driver accepted `integrate_trial: Callable[[str], object]` in place of the Phase 5 placeholder `integrator_commit_factory` parameter; in Phase 8c that hook moved to `run_orchestrator_iteration`. Eval-manifest bytes are deterministic (sorted keys, `indent=2`, trailing newline) to make §5.3 idempotency re-derivation stable.

Author identity and `commit.gpgsign=false` are pinned per-invocation so the user's ambient git config never leaks into integrator commits.     45	At Phase 10 chunk 10d, markdown linting, JSON Schema validation, and the Python toolchain for the `eden-contracts`, `eden-dispatch`, `eden-git`, `eden-storage`, `eden-wire`, `eden-service-common`, `eden-task-store-server`, `eden-orchestrator`, `eden-planner-host`, `eden-implementer-host`, `eden-evaluator-host`, and `eden-web-ui` reference packages and services are wired up.
    46	
    47	| Command | Purpose |
    48	|---|---|
    49	| `npx --yes markdownlint-cli2@0.14.0 "**/*.md" "#node_modules" "#.venv" "#docs/archive/**" "#docs/plans/review/**"` | Lint all tracked markdown (pinned to CI's version; matches CI exactly) |
    50	| `pipx run 'check-jsonschema==0.29.4' --check-metaschema spec/v0/schemas/*.schema.json` | Validate each schema file against the Draft 2020-12 meta-schema (version pinned to CI) |
    51	| `pipx run 'check-jsonschema==0.29.4' --schemafile spec/v0/schemas/experiment-config.schema.json tests/fixtures/experiment/.eden/config.yaml` | Validate the fixture experiment config against its schema |
    52	| `uv sync` | Install/refresh the workspace virtualenv (all reference packages + services) |
    53	| `uv run ruff check .` | Lint Python (config in root `pyproject.toml`) |
    54	| `uv run pyright` | Type-check the reference Python packages |
    55	| `uv run pytest -q` | Run the full reference test suite (includes the `pytest.mark.e2e` real-subprocess test) |
    56	| `uv run pytest -m e2e` | Run only the real-subprocess Phase 8b end-to-end test |
    57	| `uv run pytest reference/packages/eden-contracts/tests/test_schema_parity.py` | Run only the schema ↔ Pydantic model parity check |
    58	| `python3 -m eden_task_store_server --store-url :memory: --experiment-id exp-1 --experiment-config tests/fixtures/experiment/.eden/config.yaml --port 0` | Run the reference task-store-server (announces `EDEN_TASK_STORE_LISTENING host=… port=…` on stdout). `--store-url` accepts `:memory:`, `sqlite:///<path>`, `postgresql://…`, or a bare path. |
    59	| `python3 -m eden_orchestrator …` / `python3 -m eden_planner_host …` / `python3 -m eden_implementer_host …` / `python3 -m eden_evaluator_host …` / `python3 -m eden_web_ui …` | Run each reference service (see each service's `README.md` for full flag list). The web-ui announces `EDEN_WEB_UI_LISTENING host=… port=…` on stdout, mirroring the task-store-server convention. Pass `--repo-path <bare-repo>` to the web-ui to enable the implementer module; omit it for a planner+evaluator deployment. |
    60	| `python3 -m eden_service_common.repo_init --repo-path <dir>` | Idempotent bare-repo seed; emits `EDEN_REPO_SEEDED sha=<hex>` (or `EDEN_REPO_ALREADY_SEEDED`). Used by setup-experiment. |
    61	| `python3 scripts/spec-xref-check.py` | Validate every `§N.M` reference in `spec/v0/*.md` resolves to a real section heading in its target chapter. Run before committing a normative spec change. |
    62	| `EDEN_TEST_POSTGRES_DSN=postgresql://… uv run pytest -q reference/packages/eden-storage/tests` | Run the parametrized backend conformance tests against a live Postgres (CI's `python-test-postgres` does this). Without the env var, postgres rows skip. |
    63	| `bash reference/scripts/setup-experiment/setup-experiment.sh tests/fixtures/experiment/.eden/config.yaml --experiment-id <id>` | Bootstrap a Compose stack for the given experiment config: generate `.env`, build the shared image, seed the bare-repo volume, capture seed SHA. |
    64	| `cd reference/compose && docker compose --env-file .env up -d --wait` | Bring up the full Phase 10b/10c stack — infra + EDEN services + web-ui on `localhost:${WEB_UI_HOST_PORT:-8090}`. Run setup-experiment first. |
    65	| `bash reference/compose/healthcheck/smoke.sh` | Run the extended Compose smoke test (mirrors the `compose-smoke` CI job — runs setup-experiment, brings the stack up, waits for orchestrator quiescence, asserts ≥3 `trial.integrated` events, ≥9 `task.completed` events, and ≥3 plan-task `task.completed` events). |
    66	| `bash reference/compose/healthcheck/smoke-subprocess.sh` | Phase 10d subprocess-mode smoke (mirrors the `compose-smoke-subprocess` CI job; layers `compose.subprocess.yaml` over the base stack and runs against the fixture's `plan.py` / `implement.py` / `eval.py`). |
    67	| `eden_planner_host --mode subprocess --experiment-config <path> --experiment-dir <path> --artifacts-dir <path>` | Run the planner host with a user-supplied long-running `plan_command` (read from the experiment-config YAML; subprocess exchanges JSON-line messages per [`spec/v0/reference-bindings/worker-host-subprocess.md`](spec/v0/reference-bindings/worker-host-subprocess.md)). |
    68	| `eden_implementer_host --mode subprocess --experiment-config <path> --experiment-dir <path> --repo-path <path>` | Per-task subprocess implementer; cwd = freshly-created git worktree at `parent_commits[0]`; reads outcome JSON from `EDEN_OUTPUT`. |

    72	
    73	These are listed for orientation; the tooling is not wired up yet.

    75	| Command | Lands in |
    76	|---|---|
    77	| Container-isolated `*_command` execution (sandbox per task) | Phase 10d follow-up |

    80	
    81	## Contribution conventions
    82	
    83	### Spec edits
    84	
    85	+ Every cross-component contract (wire format, state machine, invariant) is defined in `spec/` first, then implemented in `reference/`. When they disagree, the spec wins and the impl gets a bug.
    86	+ Spec chapters use RFC 2119 language: MUST, SHOULD, MAY.
    87	+ Spec versions are frozen once stable (`spec/v0/` is a single lineage; breaking changes go to `spec/v1/`).
    88	+ Schema changes must update the spec prose, the JSON Schema file, and (from Phase 3 onward) the Pydantic bindings in lockstep. CI will enforce schema ↔ model parity once both exist.
    89	
    90	### Reference implementation
    91	
    92	+ Normal code-review discipline.
    93	+ Normative behavior is tested against the conformance suite (once it exists in Phase 11).
    94	+ The reference impl is explicitly not a monopoly on correctness. A third-party implementation that passes conformance is equally valid.
    95	
    96	### Adding a new service or package with its own `tests/` directory
    97	
    98	Pytest's default `prepend` import mode names test modules by file basename, so two `tests/test_app.py` files in different packages collide on collection (`import file mismatch`). The repo's tests-dir layout is mixed — some packages (e.g. [`eden-contracts/tests/`](reference/packages/eden-contracts/tests/)) have `__init__.py` and use `from .conftest import …`; most service test dirs (e.g. [`web-ui/tests/`](reference/services/web-ui/tests/)) have no `__init__.py` and use `from conftest import …`.
    99	
   100	When adding a new tests directory:
   101	
   102	+ Use a basename that is unique across the whole `testpaths` set in [`pyproject.toml`](pyproject.toml). Grep `find reference -name 'test_*.py' -exec basename {} \;` to verify before merging.
   103	+ Do **not** add `__init__.py` to a service tests dir while another tests dir already has one — both directories become the `tests` package and the second conftest collides on `tests.conftest` import/registration. Under default `prepend` mode this surfaces as `ImportPathMismatchError`; under `importlib` mode it surfaces as `Plugin already registered under a different name`.
   104	+ Switching the whole repo to `--import-mode=importlib` looks tempting but is **not** a clean fix today: the existing eden-contracts tests use relative `.conftest` imports that depend on the `__init__.py` package layout, and importlib mode keeps that collision while removing the sys.path hack the rest of the suite relies on.
   105	
   106	If the constraint becomes load-bearing later (Phase 10 will add several more service test dirs), the cleanest fix is a three-step lockstep: rename each tests dir to a unique package name (e.g. `web_ui_tests/` instead of `tests/`), switch every bare `from conftest import …` to a package-relative `from .conftest import …`, and add `--import-mode=importlib` to pytest's `addopts`. A mechanical basename-uniqueness check in CI is also a sensible follow-up — AGENTS-only guidance is weaker than a guardrail.
   107	
   108	### Adding or extending a JSON Schema + Pydantic binding
   109	
   110	The `schema-parity` CI job is only as strong as what both sides of the test actually enforce. Several Pydantic and `jsonschema` defaults let drift through silently. When adding a new schema — or a new field type to an existing one — evaluate each of the following; reusable implementations live in [`reference/packages/eden-contracts/src/eden_contracts/_common.py`](reference/packages/eden-contracts/src/eden_contracts/_common.py).
   111	
   112	+ **Strict numeric parsing.** Top-level models set `ConfigDict(strict=True, extra="allow")`. Non-strict mode coerces `True`/`"2"` into int, but the schemas treat `type: integer` / `type: number` as strict JSON types. New models must keep `strict=True`.
   113	+ **Format assertions on both sides.** `format` keywords (`uri`, `date-time`, …) are advisory by default in both the `jsonschema` library and Pydantic. The schema-side validator wires a custom `FormatChecker` in [`tests/conftest.py`](reference/packages/eden-contracts/tests/conftest.py), and the model side uses the reusable types in `_common.py`. A new `format` keyword in any schema requires handlers on *both* sides; the `test_format_coverage` test fails loudly if the schema-side handler is missing.
   114	+ **Real date-time / URI validation, not just regex or `urlparse`.** The regex on `DateTimeStr` accepts impossible values like `2026-99-99T…Z`; an `AfterValidator` runs `datetime.fromisoformat` to reject those. `UriStr` uses `rfc3986-validator`, not `urllib.parse.urlparse`, which accepts malformed schemeful URIs (e.g., spaces in the host).
   115	+ **Null vs absent.** JSON Schema's `type: X` rejects explicit `null`, but Pydantic's `X | None = None` accepts it. Wrap every optional typed field with `NotNone` from `_common.py` so absent is accepted and explicit null is rejected.
   116	+ **Round-trip emission.** `model.model_dump(mode="json", exclude_none=True)` must re-validate against the schema. Add any new model to `tests/test_roundtrip.py` so this is checked.
   117	+ **Corpus coverage.** Parity is asserted over the fixture corpus in `tests/cases.py`. A new field type deserves at least one accept fixture and one reject fixture per constraint the schema imposes (required, pattern, enum, min/max, format, cross-field).
   118	
   119	## Commit guidelines
   120	
   121	+ Short imperative subjects (e.g., "Add event protocol chapter", "Pin task state machine").
   122	+ For pull requests, include: what the change is, which phase it advances, and any spec ↔ impl implications.
   123	
   124	## Related docs
   125	
   126	+ [`docs/naming.md`](docs/naming.md) — what EDEN is, at the concept level.
   127	+ [`docs/roadmap.md`](docs/roadmap.md) — the 13-phase build-up plan with unit-level decomposition.
   128	+ [`docs/plans/`](docs/plans/) — active implementation plans.
   129	+ [`docs/archive/`](docs/archive/) — historical reference docs.
   130	+ [`STYLE_GUIDE.md`](STYLE_GUIDE.md) — formatting and naming conventions.
   131	+ [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](CONTRIBUTING.md) — setup and PR workflow.

1	# eden-evaluator-host
     2	
     3	Reference evaluator worker: claims pending `evaluate` tasks, emits deterministic metrics matching the experiment's `metrics_schema`, and submits.
     4	
     5	## Run
     6	
     7	```bash
     8	python -m eden_evaluator_host \
     9	  --task-store-url http://127.0.0.1:8080 \
    10	  --experiment-id exp-1 \
    11	  --worker-id evaluator-1 \
    12	  --experiment-config tests/fixtures/experiment/.eden/config.yaml
    13	```
    14	
    15	Runs until SIGTERM.
    16	
    17	## Subprocess mode
    18	
    19	Pass `--mode subprocess` plus `--experiment-dir <path>`,
    20	`--repo-path <path>`, and (optionally) `--worktrees-dir <path>` to
    21	invoke a user-supplied per-task evaluate command instead of the
    22	scripted profile. The command string is read from the
    23	experiment-config YAML's `evaluate_command` key.
    24	
    25	For each evaluate task the host creates a worktree at
    26	`trial.commit_sha`, runs the command with cwd=wt and env
    27	carrying `EDEN_TASK_JSON` / `EDEN_OUTPUT` / `EDEN_WORKTREE` /
    28	`EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR`, then reads `<wt>/.eden/eval-outcome.json`
    29	(`status` + `metrics`). Metrics are validated against
    30	`metrics_schema` before submit; type mismatches route to
    31	`status=eval_error`. See the
    32	[reference binding](../../../spec/v0/reference-bindings/worker-host-subprocess.md).

541	    def claim(
   542	        self,
   543	        task_id: str,
   544	        worker_id: str,
   545	        *,
   546	        expires_at: datetime | str | None = None,
   547	    ) -> TaskClaim:
   548	        """Transition a pending task to claimed. Returns the issued claim.
   549	
   550	        Rejects if the task is not in ``pending`` state
   551	        (``04-task-protocol.md`` §3.4). Issues a fresh token.
   552	        """
   553	        with self._atomic_operation():
   554	            task = self._require_task(task_id)
   555	            if task.state != "pending":
   556	                raise IllegalTransition(
   557	                    f"cannot claim task in state {task.state!r} (must be 'pending')"
   558	                )
   559	            claimed_at = self._ts()
   560	            claim_kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {
   561	                "token": self._token_factory(),
   562	                "worker_id": worker_id,
   563	                "claimed_at": claimed_at,
   564	            }
   565	            exp = self._maybe_ts(expires_at)
   566	            if exp is not None:
   567	                claim_kwargs["expires_at"] = exp
   568	            claim = TaskClaim(**claim_kwargs)
   569	            now = self._ts()
   570	            tx = _Tx()
   571	            tx.tasks[task_id] = _validated_update(
   572	                task, state="claimed", claim=claim, updated_at=now
   573	            )
   574	            tx.events.append(
   575	                self._event(
   576	                    "task.claimed",
   577	                    {"task_id": task_id, "worker_id": worker_id},
   578	                )
   579	            )
   580	            self._apply_commit(tx)
   581	            return _deep(claim)
   582	
   583	    def submit(self, task_id: str, token: str, submission: Submission) -> None:
   584	        """Transition a claimed task to submitted, persisting the result.
   585	
   586	        Idempotent per ``04-task-protocol.md`` §4.2: a resubmit with
   587	        the current token and a content-equivalent payload MUST
   588	        succeed without mutating state; an inconsistent resubmit MUST
   589	        be rejected.
   590	        """
   591	        with self._atomic_operation():
   592	            task = self._require_task(task_id)
   593	            if task.state not in {"claimed", "submitted"}:
   594	                raise IllegalTransition(
   595	                    f"cannot submit task in state {task.state!r}"
   596	                )
   597	            if task.claim is None or task.claim.token != token:
   598	                raise WrongToken(f"token does not match current claim on {task_id!r}")
   599	            self._require_submission_kind_matches(task, submission)
   600	            self._validate_submission_ref_binding(task, submission)
   601	
   602	            if task.state == "submitted":
   603	                prior = self._get_submission(task_id)
   604	                if prior is None:
   605	                    raise IllegalTransition(

520	    def create_branch(self, branch_name: str, start_point: str) -> None:
   521	        """Create a new branch at ``start_point``. Raises if it exists."""
   522	        self._run(["branch", branch_name, start_point])

   524	    def delete_branch(self, branch_name: str) -> None:

   527	
   528	    def current_branch(self) -> str | None:
   529	        """Return the current branch name, or ``None`` if detached."""
   530	        result = self._run(["symbolic-ref", "--short", "-q", "HEAD"], check=False)
   531	        if result.returncode != 0:
   532	            return None
   533	        return result.stdout.strip() or None
   534	
   535	    def add_worktree(
   536	        self,
   537	        worktree_path: Path | str,
   538	        *,
   539	        start_point: str,
   540	        branch: str | None = None,
   541	        detach: bool = False,
   542	    ) -> Path:
   543	        """Add a worktree at ``worktree_path`` starting from ``start_point``.
   544	
   545	        Exactly one of ``branch`` or ``detach`` must be supplied. When
   546	        ``branch`` is given, a new branch is created at ``start_point``
   547	        and the worktree checks it out. When ``detach`` is True, the
   548	        worktree is a detached checkout of ``start_point``.
   549	        """
   550	        if (branch is None) == (not detach):
   551	            raise ValueError("specify exactly one of `branch` or `detach=True`")
   552	        target = Path(worktree_path).resolve()
   553	        args = ["worktree", "add"]
   554	        if detach:
   555	            args.extend(["--detach", str(target), start_point])
   556	        else:
   557	            assert branch is not None
   558	            args.extend(["-b", branch, str(target), start_point])
   559	        self._run(args)
   560	        return target
   561	
   562	    def remove_worktree(self, worktree_path: Path | str, *, force: bool = False) -> None:
   563	        """Remove a registered worktree."""
   564	        args = ["worktree", "remove"]
   565	        if force:
   566	            args.append("--force")
   567	        args.append(str(Path(worktree_path)))
   568	        self._run(args)
   569	
   570	    def prune_worktrees(self) -> None:
   571	        """Run ``git worktree prune`` to clear stale metadata."""
   572	        self._run(["worktree", "prune"])
   573	
   574	    def list_worktrees(self) -> list[WorktreeInfo]:
   575	        """Parse ``git worktree list --porcelain`` into structured records."""
   576	        result = self._run(["worktree", "list", "--porcelain"])
   577	        records: list[WorktreeInfo] = []
   578	        current: dict[str, str] = {}
   579	        for raw in result.stdout.splitlines():
   580	            line = raw.strip()
   581	            if not line:
   582	                if current:
   583	                    records.append(_worktree_record(current))
   584	                    current = {}
   585	                continue
   586	            key, _, value = line.partition(" ")
   587	            current[key] = value
   588	        if current:
   589	            records.append(_worktree_record(current))
   590	        return records

1	"""Role-specific submission payloads passed to ``Store.submit``.
     2	
     3	The wire-format task schemas (``spec/v0/schemas/task.schema.json``)
     4	pin the *task* envelope; the *submission* payload is role-specific
     5	and defined by the role contracts in ``spec/v0/03-roles.md`` §2.4,
     6	§3.4, and §4.4. These dataclasses are the store's typed view of
     7	those payloads — they are **not** wire objects; a cross-process
     8	deployment (Phase 8) will carry them as JSON over the task-protocol
     9	binding.
    10	
    11	Fields are pinned by the spec:
    12	
    13	- ``PlanSubmission``        — status + proposal_ids (set-equivalent
    14	  per 04 §4.2).
    15	- ``ImplementSubmission``   — status + trial_id + commit_sha (03 §3.4).
    16	- ``EvaluateSubmission``    — status + trial_id + metrics + optional
    17	  artifacts_uri (03 §4.4, 04 §4.2 on metrics equivalence).
    18	
    19	All three are ``frozen=True`` so callers cannot rebind fields after
    20	construction. ``submit`` still deep-copies on entry and
    21	``read_submission`` on exit, because the ``metrics`` dict on
    22	``EvaluateSubmission`` is not itself frozen.
    23	"""
    24	
    25	from __future__ import annotations
    26	
    27	from dataclasses import dataclass
    28	from typing import Any, Literal
    29	
    30	PlanStatus = Literal["success", "error"]
    31	ImplementStatus = Literal["success", "error"]
    32	EvaluateStatus = Literal["success", "error", "eval_error"]
    33	
    34	
    35	@dataclass(frozen=True)
    36	class PlanSubmission:
    37	    """Planner submission result. See ``spec/v0/03-roles.md`` §2.4."""
    38	
    39	    status: PlanStatus
    40	    proposal_ids: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    41	
    42	
    43	@dataclass(frozen=True)
    44	class ImplementSubmission:
    45	    """Implementer submission result. See ``spec/v0/03-roles.md`` §3.4."""
    46	
    47	    status: ImplementStatus
    48	    trial_id: str
    49	    commit_sha: str | None = None
    50	
    51	
    52	@dataclass(frozen=True)
    53	class EvaluateSubmission:
    54	    """Evaluator submission result. See ``spec/v0/03-roles.md`` §4.4."""
    55	
    56	    status: EvaluateStatus
    57	    trial_id: str
    58	    metrics: dict[str, Any] | None = None
    59	    artifacts_uri: str | None = None
    60	
    61	
    62	Submission = PlanSubmission | ImplementSubmission | EvaluateSubmission
    63	
    64	
    65	def submissions_equivalent(a: Submission, b: Submission) -> bool:
    66	    """Content equivalence per ``spec/v0/04-task-protocol.md`` §4.2.
    67	
    68	    The normative fields per role (§4.2):
    69	
    70	    - plan      — status + set of proposal_ids (order not significant).
    71	    - implement — status + trial_id + commit_sha.
    72	    - evaluate  — status + trial_id + metrics (as JSON values).
    73	
    74	    ``artifacts_uri`` is deliberately absent from evaluate equivalence:
    75	    §4.2 does not list it, so two submissions that agree on the
    76	    normative fields are equivalent even if they differ in
    77	    artifacts_uri, and the first submission's artifacts_uri is the
    78	    committed one.
    79	    """
    80	    if type(a) is not type(b):
    81	        return False
    82	    if isinstance(a, PlanSubmission) and isinstance(b, PlanSubmission):
    83	        return a.status == b.status and set(a.proposal_ids) == set(b.proposal_ids)
    84	    if isinstance(a, ImplementSubmission) and isinstance(b, ImplementSubmission):
    85	        return (
    86	            a.status == b.status
    87	            and a.trial_id == b.trial_id
    88	            and a.commit_sha == b.commit_sha
    89	        )
    90	    if isinstance(a, EvaluateSubmission) and isinstance(b, EvaluateSubmission):
    91	        return (
    92	            a.status == b.status
    93	            and a.trial_id == b.trial_id
    94	            and a.metrics == b.metrics
    95	        )
    96	    return False

    90	class _ListeningAnnouncer:
    91	    """uvicorn lifespan hook that prints the bound host/port to stdout.
    92	
    93	    `--port 0` asks the kernel for an ephemeral port; the orchestrator
    94	    / test harness reads the announcement to learn which port was
    95	    actually assigned without having to scrape the log formatter.
    96	    """
    97	
    98	    def __init__(self) -> None:
    99	        self.announced = threading.Event()
   100	
   101	    def __call__(self, server: uvicorn.Server) -> None:  # noqa: D401
   102	        # uvicorn Server records bound sockets on server.servers[*].sockets
   103	        # after the serve-loop starts; poll in a background thread until
   104	        # one is available, then print.
   105	        def _wait_and_announce() -> None:
   106	            for _ in range(200):
   107	                if server.started and server.servers:
   108	                    sockets = server.servers[0].sockets
   109	                    if sockets:
   110	                        sockname = sockets[0].getsockname()
   111	                        host, port = sockname[0], sockname[1]
   112	                        sys.stdout.write(
   113	                            f"EDEN_TASK_STORE_LISTENING host={host} port={port}\n"
   114	                        )
   115	                        sys.stdout.flush()
   116	                        self.announced.set()
   117	                        return
   118	                threading.Event().wait(0.05)
   119	
   120	        t = threading.Thread(target=_wait_and_announce, daemon=True)

1	"""Helpers for the task-store-server: load config, open store, build app.
     2	
     3	Factored out of ``cli.py`` so unit tests can exercise them without
     4	booting uvicorn.
     5	"""
     6	
     7	from __future__ import annotations
     8	
     9	from pathlib import Path
    10	
    11	import yaml
    12	from eden_contracts import ExperimentConfig
    13	from eden_storage import InMemoryStore, PostgresStore, SqliteStore, Store
    14	from eden_wire import make_app
    15	from fastapi import FastAPI
    16	
    17	
    18	def load_experiment_config(path: str | Path) -> ExperimentConfig:
    19	    """Parse an experiment YAML file into an :class:`ExperimentConfig`."""
    20	    with Path(path).open() as f:
    21	        data = yaml.safe_load(f)
    22	    return ExperimentConfig.model_validate(data)
    23	
    24	
    25	def build_store(
    26	    *,
    27	    store_url: str,
    28	    experiment_id: str,
    29	    config: ExperimentConfig,
    30	) -> Store:
    31	    """Open a ``Store`` backend selected by URL scheme.
    32	
    33	    ``store_url`` dispatch:
    34	
    35	    * ``:memory:`` → :class:`InMemoryStore` (non-durable).
    36	    * ``sqlite:///<path>`` → :class:`SqliteStore` at ``<path>``.
    37	    * ``postgresql://…`` (or ``postgres://…``) → :class:`PostgresStore`.
    38	    * Bare path (no scheme) → :class:`SqliteStore` for compatibility
    39	      with pre-Phase-10 callers.
    40	    """
    41	    if store_url == ":memory:":
    42	        return InMemoryStore(
    43	            experiment_id=experiment_id,
    44	            metrics_schema=config.metrics_schema,
    45	        )
    46	    if store_url.startswith("postgresql://") or store_url.startswith("postgres://"):
    47	        return PostgresStore(
    48	            experiment_id=experiment_id,
    49	            dsn=store_url,
    50	            metrics_schema=config.metrics_schema,
    51	        )
    52	    if store_url.startswith("sqlite:///"):
    53	        path = store_url[len("sqlite:///") :]
    54	    else:
    55	        # Bare-path compatibility — interpret as SQLite filesystem path.
    56	        path = store_url
    57	    return SqliteStore(
    58	        experiment_id=experiment_id,
    59	        path=path,
    60	        metrics_schema=config.metrics_schema,
    61	    )
    62	
    63	
    64	def build_app(
    65	    *,
    66	    store: Store,
    67	    shared_token: str | None = None,
    68	    subscribe_timeout: float = 30.0,
    69	) -> FastAPI:
    70	    """Build the FastAPI app that wraps ``store`` with optional auth."""
    71	    return make_app(
    72	        store,
    73	        shared_token=shared_token,
    74	        subscribe_timeout=subscribe_timeout,
    75	    )
