item.text: I’ve got enough context to start isolating concrete issues. I’m pulling exact plan line references for the behaviors that look off so the review can point back to the intended design, not just the current code.

item.aggregated_output:
96	  `.env` as `EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR`).
    97	- **Command invocation: shell mode.** The host invokes the command
    98	  with `subprocess.Popen(cmd_str, shell=True, …)` so user
    99	  expressions like `python3 ${EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR}/plan.py` expand
   100	  correctly without the host having to parse and rewrite paths.
   101	  This is acceptable because the command string comes from the
   102	  trusted experiment-config (operator-supplied at setup time), not
   103	  from any per-task / per-trial source. Documented in the protocol
   104	  README.
   105	- **cwd.**
   106	  - Planner subprocess: `cwd = experiment-dir`. One process, lives
   107	    across all plan tasks; doesn't touch git.
   108	  - Implementer subprocess: `cwd = <task worktree>`. The user's
   109	    script runs inside the per-task git worktree and can `git
   110	    commit` directly from cwd.
   111	  - Evaluator subprocess: `cwd = <task worktree>`. The trial's
   112	    commit is checked out at cwd; the user's script reads the tree
   113	    and writes metrics.
   114	- **No free-form failure surface on the wire.** The submission
   115	  dataclasses ([`reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/submissions.py`](../../reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/submissions.py))
   116	  carry only `status` + role-specific id/payload fields; there is no
   117	  `description` field. Worker-side failure context (subprocess exit
   118	  reason, malformed outcome JSON, deadline-exceeded, etc.) is
   119	  surfaced **only via the host's structured logger** (info/warning
   120	  with `task_id`, `subprocess_role`, `reason`). The worker's own
   121	  `outcome.json` may include a `description` string for operator
   122	  diagnostics, but the host does **not** propagate it onto the wire.
   123	  Expanding the wire failure vocabulary (a normative
   124	  `Submission.diagnostic` field, or a dedicated `task.diagnostic`
   125	  event) is out of scope for this chunk and would require a spec
   126	  amendment + schema change + binding update.
   127	- **Environment.** Every subprocess receives, in addition to the
   128	  host's environment:
   129	  - `EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR` — absolute path to the experiment
   130	    directory.
   131	  - `EDEN_TASK_JSON` — relative path (under cwd) to the task brief
   132	    JSON file the host wrote.
   133	  - `EDEN_OUTPUT` — relative path (under cwd) to the file the
   134	    subprocess must write its outcome JSON to.
   135	  - For implementer/evaluator: `EDEN_WORKTREE` — absolute path to
   136	    the worktree (also equals cwd; redundant for convenience).
   137	  - User-supplied env from `--*-env-file` (intended for LLM API
   138	    keys etc.).
   139	- **Fixture-config change.** The fixture's
   140	  [`tests/fixtures/experiment/.eden/config.yaml`](../../tests/fixtures/experiment/.eden/config.yaml)
   141	  is amended in this chunk:
   142	
   143	  ```yaml
   144	  plan_command: "python3 ${EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR}/plan.py"
   145	  implement_command: "python3 ${EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR}/implement.py"
   146	  evaluate_command: "python3 ${EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR}/eval.py"
   147	  ```
   148	
   149	  Old bare values (`python3 plan.py`) wouldn't have resolved from
   150	  the implementer/evaluator's worktree cwd; the explicit
   151	  `${EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR}/` makes the intent explicit and uniform
   152	  across the three roles.
   153	
   154	### D.1 Mode selection and CLI shape
   155	
   156	Each of the three host services gains a `--mode` flag:
   157	
   158	```text
   159	eden-planner-host    --mode {scripted,subprocess}  ...
   160	eden-implementer-host --mode {scripted,subprocess} ...
   161	eden-evaluator-host  --mode {scripted,subprocess}  ...
   162	```
   163	
   164	Default stays `scripted` so existing CI jobs and the merged
   165	`compose-smoke` keep working unchanged. New flags appear only in
   166	subprocess mode.
   167	
   168	All three hosts gain in subprocess mode:
   169	
   170	- `--experiment-config PATH` — already present on evaluator + web-ui
   171	  for chunk-9d/9e parity; added to planner + implementer hosts
   172	  (required in subprocess mode). The host loads the YAML and reads
   173	  the relevant `*_command`.
   174	- `--experiment-dir PATH` — host-side absolute path to the
   175	  experiment directory (required).
   176	- `--*-env-file PATH` — additional env vars to inject (KEY=VALUE
   177	  per line); intended for `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` and similar.
   178	
   179	**Planner-host subprocess flags (added):**
   180

500	not the repo-global `git worktree prune`). Because every targeted
   501	remove is restricted to the host's own subdir, two hosts running
   502	sweeps concurrently never touch each other's worktree paths or
   503	their admin entries. The bare-repo's `worktrees/<name>/` admin
   504	entry is named after the **path basename** (`<task_id>`), so as
   505	long as `task_id` values are globally unique (the orchestrator's
   506	existing `task_id` minting per chapter 04 already guarantees this)
   507	two hosts cannot collide on an admin entry either.
   508	
   509	The host's own `task_id` paths are tracked in an in-process
   510	registry; per-task cleanup at submit-time uses
   511	`TaskWorktree.remove()`, which calls `git worktree remove --force`
   512	on that exact path. Crash-recovery cleanup runs only at startup
   513	via `sweep_host_worktrees`. There is no periodic cross-host prune.
   514	
   515	### D.6 Subprocess execution and signal handling
   516	
   517	Implemented in a new `eden_service_common.subprocess` module so the
   518	three host services share one execution path:
   519	
   520	- `subprocess.Popen` with `start_new_session=True` (each subprocess
   521	  is its own process group, so SIGTERM during host shutdown can
   522	  cleanly kill the worker tree without nuking the host).
   523	- Stdout consumed on a background thread per process; lines pushed
   524	  into a thread-safe queue the main thread reads with timeouts.
   525	- Stderr consumed on a separate background thread, forwarded to
   526	  `logger.info(line, extra={"subprocess": "<role>", "task_id": …})`.
   527	- Termination ladder: `SIGTERM`, wait `--*-shutdown-deadline`,
   528	  `SIGKILL`. Same ladder used for per-task timeouts and for host
   529	  graceful shutdown.
   530	- On host receiving SIGTERM/SIGINT (existing `StopFlag` pattern),
   531	  the subprocess receives the same signal; in-flight tasks are
   532	  abandoned (no submission); claim sweeper at the next host startup
   533	  reclaims them.
   534	
   535	### D.7 Fixture experiment scripts
   536	
   537	Three new files under
   538	[`tests/fixtures/experiment/`](../../tests/fixtures/experiment/):
   539	
   540	- `plan.py` — long-running JSON-line worker that prints `ready`,
   541	  reads dispatched plan tasks from stdin, and writes one proposal
   542	  per task. Deterministic: priority equals N − i for the i-th
   543	  proposal of an N-proposal plan; `parent_commits` from
   544	  `EDEN_BASE_COMMIT_SHA`. Invoked via the amended fixture config's
   545	  `plan_command: "python3 ${EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR}/plan.py"` (see
   546	  §D.0).
   547	- `implement.py` — short-lived; reads `EDEN_TASK_JSON`, makes a
   548	  trivial blob change, runs `git add`/`git commit`, writes
   549	  `EDEN_OUTPUT` with the resulting commit SHA. Mirrors the scripted
   550	  implementer's blob shape so the existing `compose-smoke`
   551	  assertions pass without parameterization.
   552	- `eval.py` — short-lived; reads `EDEN_TASK_JSON`, emits a
   553	  deterministic `score` matching the scripted evaluator.
   554	
   555	These exist to make the protocol testable in CI without LLM
   556	secrets. Real users supply their own `*.py` (or other interpreter)

588	are **not** plumbed through Compose env — they live in the
   589	experiment-config YAML.
   590	
   591	A new named volume `eden-worktrees` is added for the implementer/
   592	evaluator's per-task worktrees (mounted at
   593	`/var/lib/eden/worktrees`); see §D.5 for why this is separate from
   594	the bare-repo volume.
   595	
   596	Compose-side flag plumbing uses `${EDEN_WORKER_MODE:-scripted}` so
   597	the scripted-mode default is unchanged. The subprocess-mode flags
   598	appear as either-or blocks in the YAML (Compose has no native
   599	conditional, so the simplest approach is two compose files merged
   600	via `compose -f compose.yaml -f compose.subprocess.yaml`; the
   601	subprocess overlay adds the new flags and bind-mounts).
   602	
   603	The compose-smoke job stays in scripted mode (the merged shape from
   604	10c) **plus** a new `compose-smoke-subprocess` matrix entry that
   605	runs the same end-to-end assertions in subprocess mode against the
   606	fixture scripts. Two CI signals: scripted protects the chunk-1–9
   607	behavior; subprocess protects this chunk's protocol.
   608	
   609	### D.9 Tests
   610	
   611	Per-package unit tests (real subprocesses where feasible — the
   612	protocol *is* a subprocess contract, so mocking the subprocess away
   613	defeats the point):
   614	
   615	- `eden-service-common/tests/test_worktrees.py` — create/HEAD/remove
   616	  and `sweep_host_worktrees` against a real bare repo (asserts that a
   617	  sweep on host-A's subdir doesn't disturb a worktree under
   618	  host-B's subdir).
   619	- `eden-service-common/tests/test_subprocess.py` — startup deadline,
   620	  per-task deadline, SIGTERM ladder, stderr forwarding, stdout JSON
   621	  parser robustness.
   622	- `eden-planner-host/tests/test_subprocess_mode.py` — fake worker
   623	  written inline (a tiny Python `-c` script) exercising: ready
   624	  handshake; one proposal; multiple proposals; `plan-error`;
   625	  protocol violation (missing `task_id`); deadline exceeded with
   626	  graceful kill; respawn after subprocess crash.
   627	- `eden-implementer-host/tests/test_subprocess_mode.py` — fake
   628	  worker that: writes a real commit + outcome (success path); writes
   629	  a commit without an outcome file (treated as error); exits
   630	  non-zero (error); writes a commit whose parent doesn't match
   631	  proposal (rejected); deadline exceeded (terminated, error
   632	  submitted); host crash mid-flow simulation (worktree pruned at
   633	  next startup).
   634	- `eden-evaluator-host/tests/test_subprocess_mode.py` — symmetric:
   635	  success, error, eval_error, metric type mismatch (rejected before
   636	  submit), deadline exceeded.
   637	- `reference/services/orchestrator/tests/test_subprocess_e2e.py` —
   638	  `pytest.mark.e2e` using the fixture's `plan.py`/`implement.py`/
   639	  `eval.py` against real subprocesses, real worktrees, real bare
   640	  repo, and the wire binding. Mirrors the existing `test_e2e.py`
   641	  shape.
   642	
   643	CI gains a `compose-smoke-subprocess` job (parallel to the existing
   644	`compose-smoke`) that runs setup-experiment in subprocess mode
   645	against the fixture scripts and asserts the same end state.
   646	
   647	### D.10 Docs
   648	
   649	- New chapter [`spec/v0/09-worker-host-protocol.md`](../../spec/v0/09-worker-host-protocol.md)
   650	  — informative, not normative — describing the subprocess protocol
   651	  so an alternative impl can be written. Marked
   652	  `Status: informative (reference convention)` in the front matter.
   653	  The conformance suite at Phase 11 will *not* require this
   654	  protocol (a different host could use a different mechanism); the
   655	  doc just records what the reference impl does.
   656	- Per-service READMEs under
   657	  [`reference/services/{planner,implementer,evaluator}/`](../../reference/services/)
   658	  gain a `## Subprocess mode` section describing the env vars and
   659	  output-file shape users must produce.
   660	- AGENTS.md / roadmap.md — current-phase summary updated to
   661	  Phase 10d, commands table gains the new flags.
   662	
   663	## Out of scope for this chunk
   664	
   665	- **Container isolation** of `*_command` subprocesses. Deferred to
   666	  a 10d follow-up sub-chunk. When that lands, the subprocess.Popen
   667	  call is replaced with a `docker run` invocation; the worktree
   668	  becomes a bind-mount; otherwise the protocol is unchanged.
   669	- **Experiment-specific image** build pipeline. Same follow-up.
   670	- **Auth-secret mounting** (Claude/Codex CLI auth dirs into the
   671	  sandbox). Same follow-up.
   672	- **Conformance test for the protocol.** The protocol is reference-
   673	  specific (`spec/v0/09-…` is informative); Phase 11 conformance
   674	  tests cover the spec-normative state machine, not the subprocess
   675	  invocation shape.
   676	- **Gitea integration.** Workers continue to use the Compose-shared
   677	  bare repo. (Same posture 10b/c took.)
   678	- **Push-driven event delivery** to subprocess workers. Plan
   679	  dispatch is host-pull (host claims, then writes to subprocess
   680	  stdin); reactive notifications about completed trials reach the
   681	  planner only via the `history` field on the next plan task. A
   682	  push-style trial-completed notification line is a Phase-12 item.
   683	
   684	## Risks
   685	
   686	- **Fixture-script drift.** The fixture `plan.py`/`implement.py`/
   687	  `eval.py` must produce the same end state as the scripted hosts
   688	  for `compose-smoke-subprocess` to pass with the same assertions.
   689	  Mitigation: factor the deterministic shape (blob payload,
   690	  metric values) into a small shared helper imported by both the
   691	  fixture scripts and `eden_service_common.scripted` so divergence
   692	  is mechanically prevented.
   693	- **`git worktree` semantics on the bare repo.** Worktrees over a
   694	  bare repo work but require care — the worktree's `.git` is a
   695	  text file pointing at the bare repo's `worktrees/<name>/`
   696	  directory. Permissions on the bare-repo volume need to allow the
   697	  host user to write there. The 10b/10c volume already grants this
   698	  to the `eden` user; verify with the new test before relying on
   699	  it.
   700	- **Worktree leak on host crash.** Mitigation in §D.5: each host
   701	  writes its worktrees under
   702	  `<worktrees_dir>/<container_hostname>/<task_id>/` and the
   703	  startup-time `sweep_host_worktrees` only operates inside that
   704	  host-private subdir using path-scoped
   705	  `git worktree remove --force`. Cross-host races are eliminated
   706	  by construction (different subdirs); cross-host sweeps do not
   707	  exist. Malformed leftovers (no `.git` link) are logged for
   708	  operator cleanup rather than blindly deleted.
   709	- **Long-running planner subprocess as a SPOF.** A crashed Claude
   710	  CLI or a stuck process kills planner availability for the whole
   711	  experiment. Mitigation: respawn-on-crash (subject to a max
   712	  respawn count) is in scope for this chunk; cross-restart session
   713	  resumption (Claude `-c` after host restart) is a user-script
   714	  concern.
   715	- **Stdout-as-protocol fragility.** Anything the user's script
   716	  prints to stdout that *looks* like a JSON event line could spoof
   717	  the protocol. Mitigation: every event line carries the host-
   718	  supplied `task_id`; the host rejects lines whose `task_id`
   719	  doesn't match the currently dispatched task. Sentinel `ready`
   720	  has no `task_id` and is only valid before the first dispatch.
   721	  Document the spoofing risk in the protocol README.
   722	- **`history` field unboundedness.** Plan tasks late in a long
   723	  experiment carry a `history` array proportional to trial count.
   724	  Mitigation for this chunk: cap at the last 50 completed trials,

228	 "objective": {"expr": "score", "direction": "maximize"},
   229	 "metrics_schema": {"score": "real"},
   230	 "history": [{"trial_id": "trial-…", "status": "success",
   231	              "commit_sha": "abc…", "metrics": {"score": 0.7}}]}
   232	```
   233	
   234	The `history` field is a flat list of completed trials available so
   235	far (an LLM planner uses it to inform the next proposal; a
   236	deterministic planner ignores it).
   237	
   238	**Worker response.** The worker writes any number of proposal lines
   239	followed by exactly one terminator:
   240	
   241	```json
   242	{"event": "proposal", "task_id": "plan-…", "slug": "p0",
   243	 "priority": 1.0, "parent_commits": ["abc…"],
   244	 "artifacts_uri": "file:///var/lib/eden/artifacts/plan-…/p0",
   245	 "rationale": "free-form text"}
   246	{"event": "plan-done", "task_id": "plan-…"}
   247	```
   248	
   249	`task_id` on every line scopes the response so a misbehaving worker
   250	can't bleed state across tasks. `rationale` is optional; if present,
   251	the host writes it to `<artifacts_dir>/proposals/<proposal_id>/rationale.md`
   252	and stamps the proposal's `artifacts_uri` accordingly (overrides any
   253	worker-supplied `artifacts_uri`). This anchors the chunk-9c "rationale
   254	markdown rendered inline" path.
   255	
   256	**Errors.** A `{"event": "plan-error", "task_id": "plan-…",
   257	"reason": "..."}` line causes the host to submit
   258	`PlanSubmission(status="error")` and continue.
   259	A protocol violation (missing required field, wrong `task_id`,
   260	deadline exceeded) causes the host to: (a) treat the current task
   261	as `plan-error`; (b) tear down and respawn the subprocess if the
   262	violation suggests state corruption (deadline + truncated stdout);
   263	(c) continue.
   264	
   265	**Stdout discipline.** The worker's stdout is consumed line-by-line
   266	on a background thread per task; non-JSON lines (or JSON missing
   267	`event`) are logged at debug and dropped. Stderr is line-buffered
   268	and forwarded to the host's logger at info, which gives users
   269	visibility into LLM agent output.
   270	
   271	### D.3 Implementer subprocess protocol
   272	
   273	The flow honors [`spec/v0/03-roles.md`](../../spec/v0/03-roles.md) §3.2
   274	step 1: the trial MUST be persisted with `status == "starting"`
   275	**before** any repository write becomes observable. This matches the
   276	chunk-9c web-ui implementer's ordering and the scripted
   277	`ScriptedImplementer._drive_starting_trial` pattern. Per-task
   278	short-lived subprocess. The host:
   279	
   280	1. Generates a host-owned `trial_id` (server-only, never round-trips
   281	   through the request surface or the subprocess; matches chunk-9c).
   282	2. **Pre-Phase-1 ref-collision guard.** Computes the canonical work
   283	   branch name `work/<slug>-<trial_id>`. If
   284	   `repo.ref_exists(f"refs/heads/{branch}")`, short-circuits with
   285	   an `ImplementSubmission(status="error", trial_id=…)` (the
   286	   trial_id is host-generated; no `create_trial` is called, so the
   287	   id is unbound — the `_reject_implement` path tolerates this per
   288	   the chunk-9c pattern). The collision context is logged but does
   289	   not appear on the wire (Submission has no free-form fields).
   290	3. **Phase 1: Persist the starting trial.** Calls
   291	   `store.create_trial(trial_id=…, proposal_id=…, status="starting")`.
   292	   No `commit_sha` yet (it's set later by `_accept_implement` once
   293	   the submission lands). Crash after this point leaves a
   294	   recoverable orphan: the chunk-1 expired-claim sweeper composite-
   295	   commits the trial to `error` atomically with the implement task
   296	   reverting to `pending`.
   297	4. **Phase 2a: Create the worktree.** Resolves
   298	   `proposal.parent_commits[0]` against the bare repo and creates a
   299	   fresh worktree at `<worktrees_dir>/<container_hostname>/<task_id>/`
   300	   checked out
   301	   to that commit via `git worktree add --detach <path> <commit>`.
   302	   (If `parent_commits` has multiple entries, the worktree is
   303	   single-parent; the user's `implement_command` is responsible for
   304	   producing a merge commit by writing one with `git commit-tree -p
   305	   <p1> -p <p2>` or equivalent. The host's job is to start the
   306	   worktree at the *first* parent.)
   307	5. **Phase 2b: Write the task brief.** Writes
   308	   `<worktree>/.eden/task.json`:
   309	
   310	   ```json
   311	   {
   312	     "task_id": "implement-…",
   313	     "trial_id": "trial-…",
   314	     "proposal_id": "proposal-…",
   315	     "proposal_slug": "p0",
   316	     "parent_commits": ["abc…", …],
   317	     "rationale_path": "/var/lib/eden/artifacts/proposals/proposal-…/rationale.md",
   318	     "branch": "work/p0-trial-…",
   319	     "output_path": ".eden/outcome.json"
   320	   }
   321	   ```
   322	
   323	   `branch` is the canonical name from step 2; the user's script
   324	   may use it but cannot override (Phase 2c uses the canonical name
   325	   regardless of what the subprocess writes back). `rationale_path`
   326	   is absolute on the host filesystem and only present if the
   327	   proposal was created with a rationale.
   328	6. **Phase 2c: Run the subprocess.** Spawns `implement_command` per
   329	   §D.0 (cwd=worktree, env includes `EDEN_TASK_JSON`,
   330	   `EDEN_OUTPUT`, `EDEN_WORKTREE`, `EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR`, plus
   331	   `--implement-env-file` content). Awaits exit within
   332	   `--implement-task-deadline`. On timeout: SIGTERM, wait 10 s,
   333	   SIGKILL.
   334	7. **Phase 2d: Read outcome.** On exit-zero, reads
   335	   `<worktree>/.eden/outcome.json`:
   336	
   337	   ```json
   338	   {"status": "success", "commit_sha": "def…",
   339	    "description": "free-form summary"}
   340	   ```
   341	
   342	   Or `{"status": "error", "description": "…"}`. The user's script
   343	   does **not** set `branch` (host owns it).
   344	8. **Phase 2e: Validation (success path):**
   345	   - `commit_sha` exists in the bare repo's object database
   346	     (`git -C <bare> cat-file -e <sha>`). The user's `git commit`
   347	     inside the worktree updates the bare repo's object DB
   348	     automatically (worktrees share the parent's `.git/objects`).
   349	   - `is_ancestor(parent, commit_sha)` for **every** parent in
   350	     `proposal.parent_commits` per spec §3.3 reachability.
   351	   - Validation failures → submit
   352	     `ImplementSubmission(status="error", trial_id=…)` (Phase 3
   353	     below). The trial ends up `error` via the store's
   354	     `_reject_implement` path, which atomically composite-commits
   355	     the `starting` trial to `error`.
   356	9. **Phase 2f: Create the work-branch ref.** `repo.create_ref(
   357	   f"refs/heads/{branch}", commit_sha)`. The pre-Phase-1 collision
   358	   guard makes this safe; an unexpected EEXIST here (race against
   359	   another host) → submit `ImplementSubmission(status="error",
   360	   trial_id=…)`; the collision context goes to the host log only.
   361	10. **Phase 3: Submit.** Calls `store.submit(...)` with retry-before-
   362	    orphan (3 attempts, exponential backoff) per the existing
   363	    Phase-8a path. On retry exhaustion, runs the same committed-state
   364	    read-back the chunk-9c implementer route uses (read_task +
   365	    read_submission + submissions_equivalent) to classify
   366	    success / orphan / conflict.
   367	11. **Cleanup.** Removes the worktree (`git worktree remove --force
   368	    <path>`). Cleanup failure logs a warning; the next host startup
   369	    runs `sweep_host_worktrees` against this host's own subdir as a
   370	    maintenance step (see §D.5; path-scoped, not the repo-global
   371	    `git worktree prune`).
   372	12. **Failure paths:** exit-nonzero → submit
   373	    `ImplementSubmission(status="error", trial_id=…)`; timeout →
   374	    same; missing `outcome.json` → same; outcome `status="error"`
   375	    → same. All four routes go through Phase 3 with the same
   376	    retry-before-orphan + read-back classification.

556	secrets. Real users supply their own `*.py` (or other interpreter)
   557	that does LLM work.
   558	
   559	The fixture's existing `tests/fixtures/experiment/.eden/config.yaml`
   560	already declares `plan_command` / `implement_command` /
   561	`evaluate_command` keys; this chunk amends their values to the
   562	`${EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR}/<script>.py` form (see §D.0). The fixture
   563	config change is mechanical and the schema-parity tests are
   564	unaffected (these keys are accepted as additional properties).
   565	
   566	### D.8 Compose updates
   567	
   568	A new variant of the stack runs in subprocess mode by setting two
   569	new `.env` keys:
   570	
   571	```text
   572	EDEN_WORKER_MODE=subprocess        # default: scripted
   573	EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR_HOST=/abs/host/path/to/experiment
   574	```
   575	
   576	`EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR_HOST` is the **host-side** absolute path to
   577	the user's experiment directory, written by setup-experiment when
   578	invoked with `--experiment-dir <path>`. Compose bind-mounts it
   579	read-only into each subprocess-mode host at
   580	`/etc/eden/experiment-dir`, and the host's `--experiment-dir` flag
   581	points at that container path. The `experiment-config.yaml` config
   582	mount stays as-is (chunk-9c/9d wiring); the host loads the YAML and
   583	reads the `*_command` keys from it.
   584	
   585	When `EDEN_WORKER_MODE=subprocess`, each host service's `command:`
   586	gains `--mode subprocess --experiment-dir /etc/eden/experiment-dir`
   587	plus the per-host subprocess flags from §D.1. The command strings
   588	are **not** plumbed through Compose env — they live in the
   589	experiment-config YAML.
   590	
   591	A new named volume `eden-worktrees` is added for the implementer/
   592	evaluator's per-task worktrees (mounted at
   593	`/var/lib/eden/worktrees`); see §D.5 for why this is separate from
   594	the bare-repo volume.
   595	
   596	Compose-side flag plumbing uses `${EDEN_WORKER_MODE:-scripted}` so
   597	the scripted-mode default is unchanged. The subprocess-mode flags
   598	appear as either-or blocks in the YAML (Compose has no native
   599	conditional, so the simplest approach is two compose files merged
   600	via `compose -f compose.yaml -f compose.subprocess.yaml`; the
   601	subprocess overlay adds the new flags and bind-mounts).
   602	
   603	The compose-smoke job stays in scripted mode (the merged shape from
   604	10c) **plus** a new `compose-smoke-subprocess` matrix entry that
   605	runs the same end-to-end assertions in subprocess mode against the
   606	fixture scripts. Two CI signals: scripted protects the chunk-1–9
   607	behavior; subprocess protects this chunk's protocol.
   608

item.text:
**Plan Adherence**

- **Bug** — [compose.subprocess.yaml](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/compose/compose.subprocess.yaml:58), [compose.subprocess.yaml](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/compose/compose.subprocess.yaml:94): Plan §D.1/§D.8 says subprocess-mode Compose wiring should carry the per-host `--*-env-file` mechanism through to all three workers. The overlay only wires `--plan-env-file`; implementer and evaluator get no equivalent mount or flag. In the real Compose deployment, that means `implement_command`/`evaluate_command` cannot receive API keys via the documented path. Fix by adding mounted env files plus `--implement-env-file` and `--evaluate-env-file`, or update the plan/docs if this was intentionally deferred.

- **Risk** — [host.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/planner/src/eden_planner_host/host.py:89): Plan §Risks says planner respawn-on-crash is in scope “subject to a max respawn count”. The loop currently respawns forever with no cap or backoff. A broken `plan_command` can thrash indefinitely. Fix by tracking consecutive startup/task failures and stopping after a configurable ceiling.

**Correctness**

- **Risk** — [cli.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/planner/src/eden_planner_host/cli.py:136): the planner sets `EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR` and then overlays the user env file on top of it. That lets `--plan-env-file` replace a host-owned reserved variable, which breaks the §D.0 command/cwd/env contract and can redirect command expansion to the wrong tree. Fix by loading the env file first and then force-writing reserved `EDEN_*` keys, or rejecting collisions.

- **Risk** — [subprocess_mode.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/planner/src/eden_planner_host/subprocess_mode.py:278), [host.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/planner/src/eden_planner_host/host.py:117): a normal `store.claim()` race on a pending plan task is treated as an unexpected planner failure and causes the long-running subprocess to be torn down. In a deployment with another planner or the Web UI, that discards accumulated planner context for a non-fault condition. Fix by catching claim-state exceptions before dispatch and skipping the task without respawning the subprocess.

**Integration**

- **Bug** — [compose.subprocess.yaml](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/compose/compose.subprocess.yaml:86), [subprocess_mode.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/implementer/src/eden_implementer_host/subprocess_mode.py:314): Plan §D.3 step 5 depends on planner-written rationale artifacts being visible to the implementer so `rationale_path` can be threaded into `.eden/task.json`. In Compose subprocess mode, the implementer container does not mount `eden-artifacts-data`, so `file:///var/lib/eden/artifacts/.../rationale.md` does not exist there and `_rationale_path_from_uri` drops the field. The fixture passes because `implement.py` ignores `rationale_path`, but real user commands won’t get the planned planner→implementer handoff. Fix by mounting the artifacts volume into `implementer-host` at `/var/lib/eden/artifacts` (read-only is enough).

**Robustness**

- **Risk** — [subprocess_runner.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/_common/src/eden_service_common/subprocess_runner.py:195), [subprocess_mode.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/planner/src/eden_planner_host/subprocess_mode.py:178), [subprocess_mode.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/implementer/src/eden_implementer_host/subprocess_mode.py:222), [subprocess_mode.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/evaluator/src/eden_evaluator_host/subprocess_mode.py:183): the plan says worker-side failure context should be surfaced through structured logs with `task_id`/role/reason, and that worker `description` fields stay in logs rather than on the wire. The implementation loses most of that context: stderr forwarding has no `task_id`, planner `plan-error.reason` is discarded, and implementer/evaluator `outcome.description` is ignored. Fix by plumbing `task_id` into subprocess log forwarding and explicitly logging `reason` / `description` before normalizing to wire submissions.

**Code Quality**

- **Nit** — [worker-host-subprocess.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/spec/v0/reference-bindings/worker-host-subprocess.md:7): the new reference-binding doc uses broken relative links (`03-roles.md`, `04-task-protocol.md`, `schemas/...`) from inside `spec/v0/reference-bindings/`. Fix them to `../03-roles.md`, `../04-task-protocol.md`, and `../schemas/...`.

**Overall Assessment**

The core host-side subprocess flows are mostly faithful to the plan, and the implementer’s `create_trial(status="starting")` ordering is correct. The main problems are in the real deployment wiring and in planner robustness: Compose subprocess mode is still missing pieces the plan treated as part of the feature, and normal operational edge cases can currently reset planner state or hide the only useful diagnostics.
